---------------------------------------------


About This Video :

in this video we have compared the health insurance policy for different companies in india and also we have tried to understand that what exactly is health insurance policy in india, we also have compared the different health insurance polices using policy bazar platform.

Now If You are looking for the best health insurance company in India? Then In this video, we have compared top health insurance plans in India 2025 including Star Health Insurance, Niva Bupa, HDFC ERGO, ICICI Lombard, Care Health, Aditya Birla Health Insurance, Bajaj Allianz, SBI General Insurance, and Tata AIG.

Choosing the right health insurance policy is important for every Indian family. Rising hospital bills, medical inflation, and unexpected emergencies make it necessary to have a strong health insurance plan with cashless hospitalization, high claim settlement ratio, and affordable premium rates.

Topics Covered in this video :
Which is the best health insurance company in India 2025
What is claim settlement ratio (CSR) and why it matters
Best policies for individuals, families, and senior citizens
Benefits like pre & post hospitalization cover, maternity benefit, critical illness rider, OPD cover, room rent limit
Comparison of private vs government insurance providers

If you are confused between Star Health vs Niva Bupa, or wondering whether HDFC ERGO is better than ICICI Lombard, this video will help you decide. We also explain how to check cashless hospital network, IRDAI claim records, and premium affordability before buying a health plan.

also we have discussed the process that how an individual can raise a health insurance claim and how exactly the health insurance policy looks like.
tags: how to buy health insurance online
best health insurance policy 2025 in india
top health insurance companies in india
star health insurance vs nipa bupa

comments.text:
@StarHealthInsurance
My cousin met with an accident in **September 2025**, and our cashless claim (Ref: **CIR/2026/231200/1485569**) was rejected without a clear explanation. We were simply told to apply for reimbursement, but no valid reason was provided for denying cashless approval.

Your customer care team has been unhelpful—they repeat the same line: *“Cashless is denied, please file for reimbursement”*—without clarifying the basis of the rejection. If we are forced to pay the hospital upfront even after paying premiums on time, what is the purpose of having health insurance?

We need a transparent explanation for the denial and assurance that the reimbursement process will not face the same unclear obstacles.

comments.text:
So much wrong/missing information.
1. You CAN port from group health policy to retail health policy, but its a 2-step process. First you're migrated to same company's individual health policy and then ported. All accumulated benefits like waiting period etc will also migrate.
2. Don't fall for 60-75% ICR. For you, HIGHER ICR IS BETTER because it means insurance is giving away maximum claims vs the premiums they collected, without capping. Tum apna dekho, company apna dekh legi. Company will sustain or not in long term is NOT YOU CONCERN. Company ke paas 100 employees hai company ka dekhne ke liye, tum apna dekho bas.
3. 50 Lakh base or 10 base + 40 super-topup depends on your finances. Take whatever suits your finances. Yes, max base is best, but don't just run after max base. Today 50 lakh insurance looks good, after 10 years it'll be too less. That time either increase super-topup or increase both base + super-topup or take another super-topup, whatever suits your finances then. Try to take super-topup from same insurance where your base insurance is from, as claim will be seamless.
4. If possible, AVOID third class insurance company like Care, star, niva etc. Only rely on official claims data on IRDAI website under 'Handbook on Indian Insurance Statistics'.
comments.author: @joereadsx1

comments.text: Totally agree with second point, paid lakhs to niva, but they are pathetically bad when requested claim. Reasonable and customary clause is scam & sham which shall not exist and is a fail by IRDAI/govt.
comments.author: @FatalFilesInc

comments.text: Thanks for the information, but I need suggestion/ guide me which company is best in terms of easy claim settle, for a female of five, that will be very kind

comments.text:
@rickba9  what do you mean by female of 5? You mean a female child of age 5? Afaik, you cannot buy a standalone policy for child of age 5.
And if you meant 'family of 5', then please see the video properly. We're not here to do your homework. If claim is genuine, every single company has to pay claim amount as per policy selected. Infact, any company in top 10 will pay instantly for any genuine claim. But if you cannot do even one bit of research for your own family, then go with HDFC ERGO.
